2603.17759.title: Harm or Humor: A Multimodal, Multilingual Benchmark for Overt and Covert Harmful Humor
2603.17759.paper_summary: Dark humor often relies on subtle cultural nuances and implicit cues that require contextual reasoning to interpret, posing safety challenges that current static benchmarks fail to capture. To address this, we introduce a novel multimodal, multilingual benchmark for detecting and understanding harmful and offensive humor. Our manually curated dataset comprises 3,000 texts and 6,000 images in English and Arabic, alongside 1,200 videos that span English, Arabic, and language-independent (universal) contexts. Unlike standard toxicity datasets, we enforce a strict annotation guideline: distinguishing \emph{Safe} jokes from \emph{Harmful} ones, with the latter further classified into \emph{Explicit} (overt) and \emph{Implicit} (Covert) categories to probe deep reasoning. We systematically evaluate state-of-the-art (SOTA) open and closed-source models across all modalities. Our findings reveal that closed-source models significantly outperform open-source ones, with a notable difference in performance between the English and Arabic languages in both, underscoring the critical need for culturally grounded, reasoning-aware safety alignment. \textcolor{red}{Warning: this paper contains example data that may be offensive, harmful, or biased.}
2603.17759.paper_summary_zh: 黑色幽默通常依賴於微妙的文化細微差別和隱含的暗示，這需要上下文推理來解釋，這對當前靜態基準提出了安全挑戰。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了一個新穎的多模態、多語言基準，用於檢測和理解有害和冒犯性的幽默。我們手動策劃的數據集包含3,000篇文本和6,000張英語和阿拉伯語的圖片，以及1,200個涵蓋英語、阿拉伯語和語言獨立（通用）上下文的視頻。與標準的毒性數據集不同，我們強制執行嚴格的標註指南：區分\emph{安全}笑話和\emph{有害}笑話，後者進一步分類為\emph{明確}（顯性）和\emph{隱含}（隱性）類別，以深入探討推理。我們系統地評估了所有模態下的最先進（SOTA）開源和閉源模型。我們的研究結果顯示，閉源模型的表現顯著優於開源模型，並且在英語和阿拉伯語之間的表現差異顯著，強調了對文化根植的、關注推理的安全對齊的迫切需求。 \textcolor{red}{警告：本文包含可能冒犯、有害或偏見的示例數據。}
2603.17759.author: Ahmed Sharshar et.al.
2603.17759.authors: Ahmed Sharshar, Hosam Elgendy, Saad El Dine Ahmed, Yasser Rohaim, Yuxia Wang
